senses.definition: Relating to or coming from the south part of a country or region.
examples: The southern coast is warmer than the northern coast.
They took a trip to the southern cities.
A southern wind blew across the valley.

senses.definition: A person from the southern part of a country; sometimes used to refer to someone from the southern United States.
examples: The Southerner spoke with a gentle drawl.
Southerners often have distinct regional traditions.
The photographer captured a portrait of a proud Southerner.

senses.definition: A person who comes from the southern part of a country, especially the southern United States.
examples: The Southerner shared stories about life on the plantation.
We met a kind Southerner who offered directions.

senses.definition: People who come from the southern part of a country.
examples: The Southerners welcomed us with a barbecue.
Two Southerners shared climate fondness about the region.

senses.definition: Superlative form indicating the most southern; used in some dialects or poetic language.
examples: They traveled to the southernest point of the coast.
The southernest town felt the strongest sea breeze.

senses.definition: To make something more southern in character or influence; to mark with southern traits (British spelling).
examples: Some writers southernise their novels to appeal to a rural audience.
The festival was southernised to reflect regional traditions.

senses.definition: Having been made more southern in character or influence.
examples: The festival was southernised to reflect regional traditions.
Architecture in the town was southernised during the renovation.

senses.definition: Third-person singular present of southernise; to make something more southern in character.
examples: The author southernises the setting to appeal to readers in the south.
The presentation southernises its imagery with southern dialect.

senses.definition: The act of making something more southern in character.
examples: The process of southernising the festival took years.
They are southernising the design with regional motifs.

senses.definition: A word, expression, or habit typical of the southern region or its people.
examples: The term is a southernism that many outsiders may not understand.
Regional southernisms include specific phrases and pronunciations.

senses.definition: Plural of southernism; multiple southern regional expressions or habits.
examples: The book catalogues various southernisms found in dialects.
Readers noted several humorous southernisms.

senses.definition: To make something more southern in character or influence.
examples: They decided to southernize the menu with regional dishes.
The film aimed to southernize its setting for authenticity.

senses.definition: Having been southernized; made more southern in character.
examples: The town was southernized by new cultural events.
The menu was southernized to reflect local tastes.

senses.definition: Third-person singular present of southernize; to make something more southern.
examples: The festival southernizes its programming each year.
The design agency southernizes the branding.

senses.definition: The act of southernizing; making something more southern.
examples: Southernizing the festival took careful planning.
They are southernizing the menu to honor regional flavors.

senses.definition: The quality of being southern; charm or style associated with the south.
examples: The southernliness of the hostess made guests feel welcome.
You can sense the southernliness in their hospitality.

senses.definition: In a southern manner or towards the south.
examples: The road bends southernly toward the coast.
They traveled southernly, enjoying warmer weather.

senses.definition: The farthest south in a group or area.
examples: The southernmost point of the island is accessible by footpath.
They took a boat to the southernmost town.

senses.definition: The quality of being southern; the characteristic traits of the south.
examples: Her songs carry a sense of southernness and storytelling.
The novel captures southernness in its setting and dialogue.

senses.definition: Plural of southernness; multiple instances of southern quality.
examples: The guide noted several regional southernnesses in the stories.
Different towns showed varying degrees of southernnesses.

senses.definition: People from the south; a plural form similar to southerner(s).
examples: The group included several Southerns who played traditional music.
Local Southerns are proud of their regional customs.

senses.definition: A plant with yellow flowers in the citrus or rue family, used in gardens and sometimes in traditional medicine.
examples: The garden has a scented bed of southernwood.
Ancient herb gardens included southernwood for fragrance.

senses.definition: Plants of the southernwood species; plural form.
examples: The hedges were trimmed with southernwoods.
They collected several southernwoods for the herb garden.

senses.definition: People who live in the southern part of a country; similar to southerners.
examples: The town welcomed its new residents, the southers.
Southers often state their hometown pride loudly.

senses.definition: A direction toward the south; a course or movement to the south.
examples: The ship steered to the southing to reach warmer waters.
They noted the southing breeze on the map.

senses.definition: Plural of southing; movements or directions toward the south.
examples: The map shows several southings along the coast.
Wind descriptions included various southings.
